source_note: Seed authoritative knowledge for dual-track RAG. Entries must be traceable to guidelines / consensus / product FAQ. NOT user memory. Replace with reviewed clinical content before any real use.

documents.title: Time in Range consensus
documents.text: International consensus recommends Time in Range (TIR) 70-180 mg/dL as a key CGM metric; a common target for many adults is TIR above 70 percent, with time below range under 4 percent. 14 days of CGM data with at least 70 percent wear time approximates 3-month glucose exposure.

documents.title: Common CGM misreads
documents.text: Compression lows (pressure-induced false low readings) can occur when lying on a sensor; first-day sensor drift and exercise can also distort readings. These are data-quality phenomena, not necessarily true hypoglycemia.

documents.title: Hypoglycemia general education
documents.text: Glucose below 70 mg/dL is generally considered low. General education emphasizes following clinician guidance and established device alarms for treatment; this knowledge base does not provide dosing or treatment instructions.
